slots.meaning: The child protagonist and narrator's POV.
slots.guidance: A single kid-relatable explorer (name or role); capable, not reckless.

slots.meaning: The hero's older sibling/companion who travels with them all night.
slots.guidance: An older, protective sibling or friend who leads on the physical legs of the journey; never a source of peril to the hero.

slots.meaning: The parent who sets the ground rules and is called on at the finale.
slots.guidance: A trusted adult who grants supervised freedom under clear safety rules; appears at the start and may appear again at the finale.

slots.meaning: The deceased relative who built the whole night's hunt as a legacy.
slots.guidance: A beloved, resourceful elder whose voice and handwriting recur throughout; her death is backstory, never depicted as harm.

slots.meaning: The family surname used as a proud, recurring callback.
slots.guidance: A short surname other characters can invoke ('a Vega would...').

slots.meaning: The whole bounded, explorable place the night's hunt happens inside.
slots.guidance: A single town/neighborhood at night, self-contained and walkable by bike; wonder over dread; no lethal hazards anywhere in it.

slots.meaning: The central hub the hero returns to between sites and after a setback.
slots.guidance: Always reachable and safe; the anchor point every route starts and ends at.

slots.meaning: The recurring moment/condition that opens the night's mystery.
slots.guidance: A repeatable, non-lethal window (an annual or nightly trigger); must be repeatable so 'next year' or 'next time' reads true.

slots.meaning: The non-lethal time pressure that closes the night's safe window.
slots.guidance: A survivable deadline: dawn/sunrise or an equivalent; being caught by it means a missed chance, never harm.

slots.meaning: The sensory sign the deadline is arriving, used at retreats.
slots.guidance: A visible, gradual cue that escalates slowly enough to retreat safely by.

slots.meaning: The name of the final destination the whole hunt converges on.
slots.guidance: A cozy, safe, named destination; the hunt's true endpoint.

slots.meaning: The opening clause of the recurring call-and-response refrain.
slots.guidance: A short, singable phrase repeated at every site's discovery; pairs with GOAL to complete the refrain ('..., then come home to {GOAL}').

slots.meaning: The ancestor's on-air nickname/handle and the family's endearment.
slots.guidance: A short, warm handle used both as a radio callsign and as an affectionate term of address.

slots.meaning: The physical device that receives the mystery broadcast.
slots.guidance: An old, inherited receiver small enough to carry on a bike; benign, no batteries/hazards implied.

slots.meaning: The recurring recorded voice/signal that drives the whole plot.
slots.guidance: A calm, patient recorded transmission; never distressing.

slots.meaning: The notebook that holds the decoding key and the ancestor's notes.
slots.guidance: A worn notebook in the ancestor's hand; the primary decoding aid.

slots.meaning: The decoding system that turns broadcast groups into place names.
slots.guidance: A simple, kid-solvable substitution grid or key.

slots.meaning: The direction-finding tool that points toward the final site.
slots.guidance: A hand-turned antenna on the device; points steadily toward GOAL.

slots.meaning: The full name of the ancestor's old amateur-radio club.
slots.guidance: A warm, community, night-shift-adjacent club name.

slots.meaning: The singular demonym for a member of CLUB_NAME.
slots.guidance: Used for 'a Nightjar', 'old Nightjars'; must derive naturally from CLUB_NAME.

slots.meaning: The story's own title-phrase, echoed inside two ending titles.
slots.guidance: A evocative two-to-three word phrase naming the whole phenomenon (the broadcast/mystery collectively).

slots.meaning: The recurring personal signature/waypoint mark the ancestor left at every site.
slots.guidance: A single plain noun (a shape or symbol) reused as a bare word so it can pluralize ('{MARKER}s') and combine with articles freely.

slots.meaning: The hub choice's destination name for site A.
slots.guidance: A short, evocative site name, distinct from the other four sites.

slots.meaning: The path from the hub to site A.
slots.guidance: A short, safe approach path/street name.

slots.meaning: Site A's commit-or-turn-back obstacle (climb the frozen cage vs. trust the other clue); names the setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; a locked, frost-slicked obstacle. Retreat is always safe. Must be retreatable.

slots.meaning: The sensory clue found by tapping the legs (the 'tap' sub-path).
slots.guidance: A benign sound cue.

slots.meaning: The small container discovered on the singing leg.
slots.guidance: An inert, safe container; no danger to open.

slots.meaning: The puzzle card found inside ROUTE_A_FIND.
slots.guidance: A hand-drawn clue card pointing to the next spot.

slots.meaning: The unlocked room holding the site's hiding spot.
slots.guidance: Safe, dusty, unremarkable; an ordinary utility room.

slots.meaning: The specific hiding container found on the shed's shelf.
slots.guidance: A light, obviously-not-really-paint container; safe to open.

slots.meaning: The single word found on site A's collectible tag; the first word of the finale's assembled sign-off phrase.
slots.guidance: One plain word, Title Case; combines with the other four TAG slots in reading order to form the finale's closing phrase.

slots.meaning: The hub choice's destination name for site B.
slots.guidance: A short, evocative site name, distinct from the other four sites.

slots.meaning: The entry point into site B.
slots.guidance: A short, always-open side entrance.

slots.meaning: Site B's commit-or-turn-back obstacle (the weakened stage floor); names the setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; a hazardous-but-survivable floor patch. Retreat is always safe. Must be retreatable.

slots.meaning: The engraved plate found under/on the named seat.
slots.guidance: A small, safe, curiously well-kept fixture.

slots.meaning: The viewing window into the projection booth.
slots.guidance: A safe way to preview the booth before entering.

slots.meaning: The single tin that stands out among the shelved reels.
slots.guidance: An inert, labeled container; safe to open.

slots.meaning: Site B's alternate positive/discovery ending name (watching the old reel instead of continuing the hunt).
slots.guidance: Positive; a shared, wondrous experience, not a physical keepsake.

slots.meaning: The single word found on site B's collectible tag.
slots.guidance: One plain word, Title Case; second word of the assembled phrase.

slots.meaning: The hub choice's destination name for site C.
slots.guidance: A short, evocative site name, distinct from the other four sites.

slots.meaning: The path from the hub to site C.
slots.guidance: A short, safe waterfront approach.

slots.meaning: Site C's risky-shortcut obstacle (rowing an unseaworthy boat); names the setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; the boat swamps in shallow water near the shore, never open water peril. Must be retreatable.

slots.meaning: The key handed over by the site's helpful adult.
slots.guidance: A warm, given-not-stolen key; the harbormaster's trust token.

slots.meaning: The single word found on site C's collectible tag.
slots.guidance: One plain word, Title Case; third word of the assembled phrase.

slots.meaning: The hub choice's destination name for site D.
slots.guidance: A short, evocative site name, distinct from the other four sites.

slots.meaning: The path from the hub to site D.
slots.guidance: A short, safe approach across open ground.

slots.meaning: Site D's commit-or-turn-back obstacle (the night alarm over the door); names the setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; a loud alarm, not a hazard. Retreat/being caught is always safe, just embarrassing. Must be retreatable.

slots.meaning: The specific archive drawer holding the site's discovery.
slots.guidance: An ordinary flat-file drawer; safe to open.

slots.meaning: The single word found on site D's collectible tag.
slots.guidance: One plain word, Title Case; fourth word of the assembled phrase.

slots.meaning: The hub choice's destination name for site E.
slots.guidance: A short, evocative site name, distinct from the other four sites.

slots.meaning: The path from the hub up to site E.
slots.guidance: A short, safe uphill approach.

slots.meaning: Site E's risky-shortcut obstacle (a steep loose-stone shortcut); names the setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; a slither-and-scrape hazard, never a fall from height. Must be retreatable.

slots.meaning: The sound the wind makes on the mast's guy-wires.
slots.guidance: A benign, musical sensory detail.

slots.meaning: The benchmark pillar beside the third anchor block.
slots.guidance: A safe, climbable-adjacent landmark.

slots.meaning: The single greased, markable bolt among the anchor's plate.
slots.guidance: An inert, hand-turnable fastener.

slots.meaning: The single word found on site E's collectible tag.
slots.guidance: One plain word, Title Case; fifth word of the assembled phrase.

slots.meaning: The hiding spot for the finale's door key.
slots.guidance: A small, safe, weatherproofed birdhouse-like container.

slots.meaning: The finale's risky-shortcut obstacle (stretching for a stuck window instead of finding the key); names its setback ending.
slots.guidance: Non-lethal; a short overreach and safe recovery, never a fall from height. Must be retreatable.
